summary: Good soundboard capture of a lively Cal Expo show with standout Wheel, Miracle and Bird Song, though the second set drags for some listeners.
review: The recording is a solid sound‑board source with a pleasant “beefy” mix and characteristic cassette hiss, offering clear bass and overall good clarity. Musically the concert shines early on – Jerry’s post‑Space Wheel and Miracle, plus an above‑average Bird Song, keep the energy high, but the second set loses momentum for several fans (notably a skipped solo in Stella Blue). Overall the show is remembered fondly by many, yet opinions are split.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is mixed (average to good) while recording quality is generally good; weighted average yields a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Cassette hiss adds vintage character; mix is strong and beefy with pronounced bass, clear overall but not immaculate – another copy of this show is noted as cleaner.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is praised for strong playing and crowd‑jammers; second set receives mixed feedback – some describe vivid, hallucinogenic moments while others call it lame due to a missed solo.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for an awesome Wheel after Space, a soaring Miracle and overall energetic presence; criticized for skipping his solo in Stella Blue.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass comes through very strong and is highlighted as a highlight of the mix.
band_member_comments.Keys: One reviewer finds Vince/keyboard work intrusive, describing it as ‘shitty’.
